patch.value: Document the create vs mutateItem dual write-path contract

create.ts writes via writeFileAtomic+appendHistoryEntry, bypassing mutateItem (the canonical post-create mutation path with locks/hooks/ownership). The split is intentional but undocumented; future cross-cutting concerns can diverge.
Inline ARCH comment in create.ts: why create bypasses mutateItem, which mutateItem concerns DO apply to create, and a checklist for adding new cross-cutting concerns; doc-only, no behavior change.

create.ts writes via writeFileAtomic+appendHistoryEntry, bypassing mutateItem (the canonical post-create mutation path with locks/hooks/ownership). The split is intentional but undocumented; future cross-cutting concerns can diverge.

value.author: sdk-docs-subagent
value.text: CODE RESEARCH FINDINGS: Two distinct write paths exist for items. CREATE PATH (src/cli/commands/create.ts:1871-1942): (1) acquireLock; (2) writeFileAtomic(itemPath); (3) createHistoryEntry + appendHistoryEntry — if appendHistoryEntry fails, itemPath is removed and the error re-thrown (rollback); (4) two runActiveOnWriteHooks calls: op=`create` on item file, then op=`create:history` on history file; (5) recordAfterCommandAffectedItem; (6) releaseLock. No `item_store_write` service override — the create path does NOT support the service-level write interception available in mutateItem. Before-document is always an empty/zeroed ItemDocument. MUTATE PATH (src/core/store/item-store.ts:347-535): (1) prepareLockedItem (locateItem + acquireLock + readLocatedItem [fires onRead hook] + ownership check); (2) enforceHistoryStreamPolicyForItem; (3) build before/after canonical documents; (4) runActiveServiceOverride('item_store_write') — full service override on item write path; (5) writeFileAtomic(effectiveTargetItemPath) unless skip_write=true; (6) old-format file removal if path changed; (7) createHistoryEntry + appendHistoryEntry — if appendHistoryEntry fails, item file is rolled back; (8) two runActiveOnWriteHooks calls: op=params.op on item file, then op=`${params.op}:history` on history file; (9) recordAfterCommandAffectedItem; (10) releaseLock. KEY INVARIANTS: (a) create does NOT fire onRead hooks on the item (it doesn't read an existing item); mutateItem fires onRead via readLocatedItem. (b) Only mutateItem goes through `item_store_write` service override. (c) Both paths guarantee: lock held for entire write+history sequence; history appended after file write; on history failure, item file is rolled back; both onWrite hooks fire post-commit (after history succeeds). (d) changed_fields sentinel values: `["imported"]` for imports, `["restored"]` for restores, `["deleted"]` for deletes. (e) Extension authors writing onWrite hooks receive: path, scope, op, item_id, item_type, before, after, changed_fields for both create and mutate paths. Documented in docs/EXTENSIONS.md under new section `Item Write-Path Contract`.

value.author: sdk-docs-subagent
value.text: The `item_store_write` service override is ONLY available on the mutateItem path (updates, closes, claims, etc.) — it is not invoked during item creation. If an extension needs to intercept create writes, it must use the `onWrite` hook (op=`create`) instead. The `history_append` service override (in core/history/history.ts:154) IS available for both paths, since both call appendHistoryEntry.

patch.value: Shipped in pm-at83 (sub-agent sdk-docs). Documented the create vs mutateItem dual write-path contract in docs/EXTENSIONS.md new section 'Item Write-Path Contract': create-path execution order (acquireLock → atomic write → createHistoryEntry/appendHistoryEntry with rollback → onWrite create + create:history → releaseLock; no onRead, no item_store_write override), mutateItem-path order (locate → lock → read[fires onRead] → ownership check → history-stream policy → mutate callback → item_store_write service override [path redirect / replace content / skip_write] → atomic write → history with rollback → onWrite ×2 → releaseLock in finally), shared invariants (atomic writes, lock spans write+history, history always after item file, onWrite only after both committed, hook failures non-fatal), and that history_append override is on both paths while item_store_write is mutateItem-only. All file:line-verified against src/cli/commands/create.ts, src/core/store/item-store.ts, src/core/history/history.ts.
